description_long: This is a modern windmill, commonly known as a wind turbine, featuring a tall, cylindrical tubular tower supporting a horizontal-axis rotor with three long, slender blades. The blades are symmetrically spaced and attached to a central hub, which is connected to a rectangular nacelle at the top of the tower. The nacelle houses the mechanical and electrical components necessary for converting wind energy into electricity. The structure is predominantly a smooth, light gray color, giving it a sleek and industrial appearance. The overall design is streamlined for efficiency and minimal air resistance, with no visible external ladders or platforms. This type of windmill is typically found in open urban parks, near highways, or on the outskirts of neighborhoods to harness wind energy for sustainable power generation.
description: A tall, sleek wind turbine with three long blades and a smooth, light gray tubular tower supporting a rectangular nacelle at the top.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the full face of the three-bladed rotor and the entire height of the tower.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thin profile of the blades and the rectangular nacelle atop the tower.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the nacelle and the symmetrical arrangement of the blades.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the blade profile and the nacelle's rectangular shape.
